text.title: QA Session-Based Test Management Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-session-based-test-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Session duration is 60-120 min, default 90 min. Going over forfeits the data.
rule.rationale: Cognitive fatigue past ~90 min reduces signal-to-noise; bounded sessions yield comparable reports.

rule.statement: Each session has a one-paragraph charter naming mission, scope, and deliverable. No 'just explore' sessions.
rule.rationale: Without a mission the session has no falsifiable result.

rule.statement: Session report breaks time into T (test design/execution), B (bug investigation), S (setup/admin). T should be ≥50%.
rule.rationale: Low T-ratio means the session was overhead-bound; tracking forces process improvement.

rule.statement: Findings classified as bugs (defect), issues (questions/risks), or tasks (work to do). Mixed classification is rejected.
rule.rationale: Mixing types means the session report cannot drive distinct workflows (triage / discussion / planning).

rule.statement: Session report is committed to the repo (or wiki with a stable URL) in the canonical schema.
rule.rationale: Ephemeral chat-only reports vanish; committed reports compound.

rule.statement: Each session ends with a 10-min debrief between tester and lead; debrief notes attached to the report.
rule.rationale: Without debrief, surprises die in the tester's head.

rule.statement: If context is regulated-scripted-only OR team has <2 testers OR no UI exists, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: SBTM is wasted overhead in those configurations.
